question_text: A cyclist travels 50 metres in 5 seconds, then 150 metres in the next 10 seconds. Calculate the average speed for the entire journey and explain whether the cyclist was accelerating.
correct_answer: Average speed = 13.3 m/s (or 40/3 m/s). Yes, the cyclist was accelerating because the speed in the second section (15 m/s) is greater than the first section (10 m/s).
explanation: Total distance = 50 + 150 = 200 m. Total time = 5 + 10 = 15 s. Average speed = 200 ÷ 15 = 13.3 m/s. First section speed: 50 ÷ 5 = 10 m/s. Second section speed: 150 ÷ 10 = 15 m/s. Since speed increased, acceleration occurred.
